touches.ai_summary: Cold email sent to Theo Collins introducing our platform's pipeline visibility capabilities. No response received.
LinkedIn connection request sent with brief message about helping RevOps teams improve forecast accuracy. No response.
Attempted cold call to Theo Collins. Call went to voicemail, left message about pipeline visibility solution.
Follow-up LinkedIn message sharing case study about SaaS company improving forecast accuracy. No response.
LinkedIn message referencing LoopCube's recent growth and challenges scaling RevOps processes. No response.
LinkedIn message with video walkthrough of pipeline visibility dashboard. Theo responded agreeing to a quick call.

Initial qualification call with Theo. Discussed current challenges with pipeline visibility and manual reporting processes. Theo mentioned they're evaluating Chorus but concerned about implementation complexity. Expressed interest in seeing a demo.

questions_asked.question: What's driving your search for a pipeline visibility solution right now?
questions_asked.answer: We're scaling quickly and our current manual reporting is breaking down. Leadership needs real-time visibility into our pipeline health.

questions_asked.question: What other solutions are you currently evaluating?
questions_asked.answer: We've been looking at Chorus, but honestly their setup seems pretty heavy for our team size.

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up qualification call to dive deeper into technical requirements and current tech stack. Theo shared that they're using Salesforce and need seamless integration. Discussed timeline - they want to have something in place before Q3.

questions_asked.question: How quickly can you get this implemented? We need something running before Q3 planning.
questions_asked.answer: For your use case, typically 2-3 weeks from contract signature to full deployment. We can prioritize your pipeline visibility dashboards first.

questions_asked.question: Beyond you, who else would need to be involved in evaluating this?
questions_asked.answer: Honestly, it's mostly my call. I own the RevOps stack. I'd probably loop in our VP Sales for final approval on budget, but that's usually straightforward.

touches.ai_summary: Quick check-in call attempt before demo. Theo didn't answer, left voicemail confirming demo time.
Pre-demo email sent with agenda and discovery recap. Theo responded confirming attendance and asking if we could focus extra time on the forecasting accuracy features.

questions_asked.question: Can we spend extra time on the forecasting accuracy features during the demo?
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely - I'll adjust the agenda to give that section more depth and show you some predictive analytics capabilities.

next_steps: Prepare customized demo focused on forecasting

touches.ai_summary: LinkedIn message sent sharing article about RevOps best practices. No response (Theo likely focused on upcoming demo).

Full product demo with Theo. Walked through pipeline visibility dashboard, forecasting features, and Salesforce integration. Theo was engaged and asked detailed questions about automation capabilities. He mentioned their 8-person sales team needs better activity tracking. Noted Chorus requires more manual configuration than he wants to deal with.

questions_asked.question: How does the automation tier work? Can we set custom triggers for pipeline alerts?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, the unlimited automation plan gives you custom trigger configuration, automated reporting, and our AI-powered deal health scoring.

questions_asked.question: What's your current process for tracking rep activity and correlating it to deal outcomes?
questions_asked.answer: Honestly, it's pretty manual. We export Salesforce data weekly and I build reports in Excel. It's not scalable.

questions_asked.question: Compared to Chorus, how much manual setup are we talking about?
questions_asked.answer: We're designed for faster deployment. Most automation templates are pre-built - you'd be configuring, not building from scratch.

next_steps: Schedule follow-up to address any technical questions

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email after demo with recording, pricing breakdown for 8 seats on unlimited automation plan ($6,400 ACV), and case study. Theo responded asking about contract flexibility and whether pricing could flex if they add more reps.

questions_asked.question: Is there flexibility to add more seats mid-contract if we grow the team?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we can add seats anytime with prorated billing. Many customers start with core team and expand within the first quarter.

touches.ai_summary: Proposal review call with Theo. Walked through pricing, contract terms, and implementation timeline. Theo asked about discount options and mentioned he needs to get VP Sales sign-off. Expressed some hesitation about budget timing but confirmed the business case is clear.

questions_asked.question: Is there any flexibility on pricing? We're a pretty lean team budget-wise.
questions_asked.answer: Let me see what I can do. For an annual commitment and quick close, I could potentially offer 10% off. Would that help get this prioritized?

questions_asked.question: What does the approval process look like with your VP Sales?
questions_asked.answer: Pretty simple - I'll send over the proposal and business case. He usually trusts my stack decisions, especially at this price point.

objections: Budget timing is tight this quarter - we might need to push to next quarter
next_steps: Theo to review with VP Sales by end of week

touches.ai_summary: Scheduled negotiation call with Theo. Call was rescheduled last minute - Theo messaged that VP Sales had a conflict and asked to move to next week.
touches.reschedule_reason: prospect asked to push by a week

touches.ai_summary: Rescheduled negotiation call with Theo. Discussed final contract terms and implementation timeline. Theo confirmed VP Sales is supportive but wanted to understand total first-year costs including implementation. Some concern about switching costs from current manual process. Asked about payment terms flexibility.

questions_asked.question: What's the total first-year investment including implementation and any setup fees?
questions_asked.answer: With the 10% discount, your annual cost is $5,760. Implementation is included at no additional charge - that's part of our standard onboarding.

questions_asked.question: Can we do quarterly payments instead of annual upfront?
questions_asked.answer: We can structure quarterly payments, though the discount would drop to 5% instead of 10%. Annual upfront unlocks the best pricing.

objections: VP Sales wants to make sure we're not over-investing before proving ROI with the current team size
next_steps: Send updated proposal with payment options
Theo to get final VP approval this week

touches.ai_summary: LinkedIn message from Theo thanking rep for the tailored proposal and confirming he's moving forward with final internal approval. Positive signal but still pending VP sign-off.
next_steps: Follow up early next week on VP approval status
